title: Ritsukos official degree
wikitext:
[]: Ritsuko's official degree             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 12, 2009 2:49 pm If we ever head a chance to peek into Ritsuko's diploma, what is she, officially? Metaphysical biologist or...?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Oct 12, 2009 2:51 pm Is it stated what degree Naoko has? I bet its teh same.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Oct 12, 2009 2:54 pm Same field as her mother I'd assume.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Oct 12, 2009 4:33 pm "Mothafuckin' genius physical mathsmith: Voice of a Mothafuckin' generation."

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 12, 2009 4:39 pm She's a Doctor of Inane Technobabble with honors in Unspeakable Horrors. She almost didn't graduate, but Gendo pulled some strings to get the professional and scientific ethics course requirements pulled. Her thesis was over 700 pages of black marker streaks, censored as a matter of international security, though a few random words were left unmasked... and, incidentally, they read collectively as a rather lewd suggestion for an evening's worth of entertainment.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 12, 2009 5:37 pm She has a degree in "whatevertheplotdemandsology". Why did I even bother to ask? I still think it's something remotely connected to neuroscience, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Mon Oct 12, 2009 7:12 pm I'd say a Cert II in I.T and a magical computer

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Oct 12, 2009 9:30 pm She wanted to go for her PHD in horribleness but her mom made her change her major until she got one in Metaphysical biology.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Oct 12, 2009 10:51 pm PHD in blonde

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Mon Oct 12, 2009 11:36 pm NemZ wrote: She's a Doctor of Inane Technobabble with honors in Unspeakable Horrors. She almost didn't graduate, but Gendo pulled some strings to get the professional and scientific ethics course requirements pulled. Her thesis was over 700 pages of black marker streaks, censored as a matter of international security, though a few random words were left unmasked... and, incidentally, they read collectively as a rather lewd suggestion for an evening's worth of entertainment.
